mnemonics: CLOUD CAP: C - Cost Control, L - Load Fluctuations, O - On-Demand Scaling, U - Usage-Based Billing, D - Dynamic Resources; C - Capacity Planning, A - Agility, P - Performance
keyPoints: Cloud elasticity allows for on-demand scaling to handle variable workloads, reducing the need for over-provisioning.
A key use case is handling unpredictable traffic spikes (e.g., e-commerce sales, viral content) without service degradation.
The shift from CapEx to OpEx model means paying for only the resources consumed, improving cost efficiency for non-steady-state workloads.
commonMistakes: Treating cloud resources as infinite and failing to implement proper auto-scaling policies and budget alerts.
Performing 'lift-and-shift' migrations without re-architecting applications to be cloud-native, missing the cost and performance benefits.
Underestimating the complexity of data transfer costs and egress fees, which can lead to unexpectedly high bills.
